title: Illustrative Rendering in Team Fortress 2
creator: Jason Mitchell, Moby Francke, and Dhabih Eng
description: Valve’s paper explains how concept art, character silhouettes, material response, and a custom shading model work together to produce Team Fortress 2’s illustrative visual style.
useful: A strong case study in translating art direction into technical rendering decisions instead of applying style only as surface decoration.
audience: Technical artists, character artists, and graphics programmers connecting a visual target to rendering code.
learning: Connect silhouette design with gameplay readability
Identify shading choices behind an illustrative style
Treat art direction as a cross-disciplinary system
